_readme: Annotated template for the Teacher Dashboard's 'Upload JSON Exam' panel (Create tab). Fields starting with an underscore, like this one, are documentation only — the system ignores them, so it is safe to leave them in or delete them. Full field-by-field rules live in backend/src/assignments/dto/assignment-json.dto.ts. Use 'Download Example (Grade 7 Maths)' next to this button to see a complete, realistic exam built the same way.
title: Sample Assignment Title
description: A short summary shown to students before they open the exam (optional).
subject: Mathematics
_type_note: One of: TEACHER_MARKED | AUTO_MARKED | PRACTICAL. Optional — defaults to TEACHER_MARKED.
_timeAllowedMinutes_note: Optional. If set, students see a countdown timer and are auto-submitted when it hits zero. Omit for an untimed assignment (students just see a stopwatch).
_totalMarks_note: Optional — if you omit this field entirely, it is computed automatically as the sum of every question's 'points' across every section. If you DO set it, it must exactly equal that sum or the upload will be rejected with a clear error telling you the mismatch.
instructions: Read each question carefully. Show all working for full marks on essay/structured questions. Calculators are not allowed unless stated.

sections.name: Section A: Multiple Choice
sections.description: Choose the single best answer for each question.

questions.hint: Think of it as 7 x 8 = 7 x (10 - 2).
questions._note: MULTIPLE_CHOICE: 'options' needs 2-8 choices; 'correctAnswer' must be an exact copy of one of them.
TRUE_FALSE: correctAnswer must be exactly "true" or "false" (lowercase string, not a boolean).

questions.questionText: The value of pi rounded to two decimal places is ____.
questions._note: FILL_BLANK: correctAnswer is matched case-insensitively and ignoring leading/trailing spaces.

sections.name: Section B: Matching & Ordering
sections.description: Follow the instructions for each question.

questions.questionText: Match each shape to its number of sides.
questions._note: MATCHING: options.left and options.right must be equal-length arrays. correctAnswer keys are LEFT-item indexes (0-based, as strings); values are the matching RIGHT-item index. Above: left[0] 'Triangle' -> right[3] '3', left[1] 'Square' -> right[0] '4', left[2] 'Pentagon' -> right[1] '5', left[3] 'Hexagon' -> right[2] '6'.

questions.questionText: Arrange these numbers from smallest to largest.
questions._note: ORDERING: 'options' is the scrambled order students first see; 'correctAnswer' is the exact same items, reordered correctly.

sections.name: Section C: Structured / Essay
sections.description: Show all your working for full marks.

questions.questionText: A shop sells pencils at KES 15 each. If Amina buys 8 pencils and pays with a KES 200 note, how much change does she get? Show your working.
questions.hint: Work out the total cost first, then subtract from 200.
questions._note: ESSAY: no correctAnswer/options — always graded manually by the teacher under 'To Grade'. Students automatically get a rich-text editor plus an optional collapsible working-space area.

questions.questionText: Take a photo of your completed diagram from the practical session and upload it here.
questions._note: FILE_UPLOAD: also no correctAnswer/options and always graded manually.
